About.metaDescription: The story behind X-Glass, who maintains it, and which Fujifilm lenses are covered — X-mount first, with G-mount also being added. Built and maintained solo by an independent engineer, with open-source web code.
About.backgroundBody1: As a Fujifilm shooter myself, picking a lens has always meant running into the same friction — looking for a fast 50mm-equivalent prime, or a do-it-all travel zoom: what lenses are actually out there that fit? The Fujifilm X-mount lens ecosystem has grown from a handful of native options in its early days into today's landscape of dozens of brands and hundreds of lenses. Yet specs are scattered across brand websites and various platforms in inconsistent formats, making meaningful cross-brand comparison a slow, frustrating exercise. X-Glass was built to pull all of that fragmented information into one place — a normalized, comparable database that lets the data speak for itself. Which one to buy is still up to you. X-Glass started as X-mount only; G-mount (medium format) was added later in response to user requests, and remains at an early stage — coverage is limited to Fujifilm's first-party GF lineup, with cross-brand reach and comparability still far less mature than on the X-mount side.
About.backgroundBody2: I'm a front-end engineer, and I currently develop and maintain X-Glass entirely on my own. I've always held a deep conviction about building a product that doesn't compromise on engineering quality, user experience, or visual craft — and that conviction is, in no small part, what made this project possible.
About.coverageBody: Coverage depth and discontinued lens inclusion vary by brand. Adapted lenses from other mounts are out of scope.
About.coverageLegend: ✓ Covered · ◐ Partial · ○ Under evaluation · — No plans
About.coverageSuggest: Missing a lens you're looking for?
About.dataAccuracyIntro: All data in X-Glass is produced by two independent pipelines. The spec pipeline collects and structures lens specifications from official brand websites. The pricing pipeline independently samples reference prices from e-commerce storefronts. The two are fully isolated, each with its own data sources and quality control process.
About.dataSpecPoint1Title: Data comes from official brand websites only
About.dataSpecPoint1Body: Canonical spec values are collected from brand official sites. Third-party material is not used as a source for published values.
About.dataSpecPoint2Title: Deterministic fields are computed in code
About.dataSpecPoint2Body: Focal length, aperture, series name, and other fields strictly derivable from the lens model name are all computed in code. Fields requiring semantic understanding are extracted by an AI agent from the collected official text — every value is traceable back to its source.
About.dataSpecPoint3Body: Pipeline stages execute in a fixed order. Each step uses only the previous step's output; intermediate stages cannot consult external sources or revise prior conclusions.
About.dataSpecPoint4Body: A separate AI without access to the pipeline's context samples and compares against published reviews before publish, to surface possible source or extraction errors. These third-party materials are used only for audit, never as canonical data sources.
About.dataPipelineIntro: Spec data is produced through a multi-stage pipeline:
About.pipeline0Desc: Scripts index lens listings from each brand's official website
About.pipeline1Desc: AI Agents collect official lens descriptions and raw spec text from manufacturer sources
About.pipeline2Desc: Script rules compute deterministic fields; AI Agents derive semantic fields
About.pipelineRDesc: Maintainer reviews each entry and applies necessary corrections
About.pipelinePDesc: Schema validation, normalization, independent cross-check, and final merge
About.dataUpdateNote: The database is updated when new lenses launch or when existing data needs correction.
About.dataVersionNote: The version number and last-updated timestamp in the footer reflect the latest published data release.
About.dataPricingPoint1Body: Pricing has its own sampling sources and collection process, kept separate from the spec pipeline. It is meant to give rough purchase context, not a live quote.
About.dataPricingPoint2Body: Prices come from brand-operated e-commerce. When a brand has no unified global storefront, the US storefront is used as a reference.
About.dataPricingPoint3Body: When no official listing is available, used market listings are sampled, and a median of multiple listings is computed to reduce outlier impact — though used prices still vary substantially with condition and timing, so treat them as a rough reference.
About.dataPricingPoint4Body: Prices reflect the state at sampling time and are not continuously updated.
About.dataGitHubCardBody: Detailed Mermaid diagram of data flow, branching, and human/AI nodes.
About.disclaimerAccuracy: X-Glass data is sourced from public materials and refined through manual review. We make every effort to keep specs accurate and up to date, but errors and delays are inevitable. Always verify key specifications on the manufacturer's official page before making a purchase decision.
About.disclaimerLiability: X-Glass is not responsible for any decisions made based on information presented here.
About.disclaimerIndependent: X-Glass is an independent third-party tool and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by Fujifilm or any lens manufacturer.
About.disclaimerCopyright: Product images and brand trademarks are the property of their respective owners. If you are a rights holder and believe your content has been used improperly, please contact xglass@sentacraft.com.
About.privacyBody: X-Glass does not require registration and does not collect personal information.
About.privacyAnalytics: This site uses Cloudflare Analytics to collect aggregated, anonymous data — such as page views and feature usage — solely to help improve the product. No personally identifiable information is collected.
About.donationBody: X-Glass is open source on GitHub. Every spec in this database went through tedious data extraction and manual cross-checking. If it helped you find the right lens — or saved you some time — consider supporting the project.
About.donationComingSoon: Sponsorship options are being set up — check back soon.
About.ackBody: Over the past year, large language models have shown capabilities that once felt out of reach. I could not have imagined completing a project at this scale on my own — let alone shipping it in under a month. Without large language models and AI coding agents, this project might have stayed an idea forever. I am grateful to live in an era where the technology dividend is real, and where a spark of inspiration can actually become something in the hands of a solo developer.
About.ackStory1Body:
Iris is X-Glass's resident character — the aperture animation on the home page that opens and closes with each tap.

Building Iris required Claude to first study the mechanical principles behind petal-shaped aperture blades — working through structural diagrams, motion logic, and kinematics documentation before writing a single line of code. What it produced is a genuine three-body constraint kinematic engine: forward and inverse kinematics solved analytically in real time, aperture openings computed from arc-intersection geometry. I had no background in mechanism kinematics whatsoever. The entire system was built from scratch.

Iris is more than an animation. Like Clippy once lived in the corner of Microsoft Office, Iris is X-Glass's resident character — accompanying every visitor with a quiet personality of its own.
About.ackStory2Body:
Gemini shaped several key design decisions throughout the project. When building the Filter Panel, it established the interaction language for single- and multi-select controls so users could understand a control's behavior at first glance. On the lens list, it refined card layout and spacing across screen sizes, keeping the information hierarchy clear on every device. For the share poster, it pushed the design toward something genuinely clean, restrained, and visually considered.

As an engineer, I could feel when something in the design was off — but rarely knew why. Gemini gave me the vocabulary to diagnose design problems, and the understanding to know why the fix worked. The site reached a level of craft I couldn't have gotten to on my own.
About.ackClosing: My deepest gratitude to the AI collaborators whose contributions were decisive in making this project a reality.

Feedback.descriptionDataIssue: Wrong spec, broken image, typo, missing lens — tell us what you found. It goes straight to the maintainer.
Feedback.descriptionGeneral: Thoughts, bug reports, and feature ideas are all welcome.
Feedback.contentRequired: Add either a suggested correction or a description.
Feedback.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe the issue with as much detail as you'd like…
Feedback.successBody: Every piece of feedback helps us make the data more accurate and complete.
Feedback.error: Something went wrong. Please try again in a bit.

Install.metaDescription: Install X-Glass to your phone home screen or desktop. App-like launch, works offline.
Install.headline: Add X-Glass to your home screen
Install.subheadline: Faster access, full-screen experience, works offline.
Install.installedBody: X-Glass is already installed on this device.
Install.promptBody: X-Glass is a web app that also supports installation as a Progressive Web App (PWA) — no App Store needed. Add it to your home screen or app launcher for one-tap access and a more native feel.
Install.iosStep1: Tap the Share button (the square with an arrow) at the bottom of Safari
Install.iosStep2: Scroll down and tap "Add to Home Screen"
Install.macosStep1: Click the Share button (the square with an arrow) in the Safari toolbar
Install.genericBody: X-Glass is a web app that also supports installation as a Progressive Web App (PWA) — no App Store needed. Open your browser menu and look for "Add to Home Screen" or "Install app".

Pricing.disclaimerBody: Actual prices may vary significantly due to retailer, promotions, condition, and market dynamics — please do verify the exact price before purchasing.
Pricing.rowWarn: please do verify the latest price
Pricing.usedReason: No new-price listing was found in official channels; showing a used-market reference instead.
